name: Beautiful Fibonacci Problem
statement:
The well-known Fibonacci sequence $F_0, F_1, F_2,\ldots $ is defined as follows:
•  $F_0 = 0, F_1 = 1$.
•  For each $i \geq 2$: $F_i = F_{i - 1} + F_{i - 2}$.
Given an increasing arithmetic sequence of positive integers with $n$ elements: $(a, a + d, a + 2\cdot d,\ldots, a + (n - 1)\cdot d)$.
You need to find another increasing arithmetic sequence of positive integers with $n$ elements $(b, b + e, b + 2\cdot e,\ldots, b + (n - 1)\cdot e)$ such that:
•  $0  \lt  b, e  \lt  2^{64}$,
•  for all $0\leq i  \lt  n$, the decimal representation of $a + i \cdot d$ appears as substring in the last $18$ digits of the decimal representation of $F_{b + i \cdot e}$ (if this number has less than $18$ digits, then we consider all its digits).
input: The first line contains three positive integers $n$, $a$, $d$ ($1 \leq n, a, d, a + (n - 1) \cdot d  \lt  10^6$).
output:
If no such arithmetic sequence exists, print $-1$.
Otherwise, print two integers $b$ and $e$, separated by space in a single line ($0  \lt  b, e  \lt  2^{64}$).
If there are many answers, you can output any of them.
